sections.paragraph:
τρίζω (intr. Hom., Hdt. et al., likewise Am 2:13 Aq; Is 38:14 Sym. [fut. τρίσω]) orig. ‘utter a shrill cry’, of sounds made by birds (Hom. et al.), then of a variety of sounds including creaking of mobile objects, also of teeth that grind (Epicharmus, Fgm. 21 Kaibel, Com. Gr. Fgm. p. 94, in Athen. 10, 411b), trans. in the only place where it occurs in our lit. gnash, grind τρίζειν τοὺς ὀδόντας gnash or grind the teeth (Ps.-Callisth. 3, 22, 13 [twice]; Cyranides p. 46, 5; TestSol 12:2) Mk 9:18. S. B-D-F §148, 1.—DELG. M-M.

intr. intr. = intransitive

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

fut. fut. = fut.

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

trans. trans. = transitive

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

Cyranides Cyranides , a compilation relating to magical properties of stones, plants, and animals; perh. antedating I/II a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1015
